what_it_does: It is an implementation guide that lays out the engineering plan for adding a concurrent-cognition layer on top of an existing node-graph ledger substrate, organized as work groups G0–G9, with governing principles, corrected design decisions, file territory, and gates.
observations: It is Document 2 of a triad and focuses on HOW to make each Completion-Criteria group true.
It is hardened by Round-1 review and treats review/R1-FOLD.md as binding, with corrections inlined.
It declares that the cognition layer should be a thin driver over the existing node-graph substrate, not a parallel system.
Injection is specified as a net-new ref-read at run://<turn>/<role>, not via existing operator-notebook resolution or swarm://.
chat_parts is framed as a hot-path refactor of suite.py chat() that extracts a shared core, not an additive wrapper.
Parallel dispatch is placed in _run_swarm / the in-turn part runner, not in the shared runtime/scheduler.py.
The concurrency budget is tied to max_num_seqs (=16) plus KV-pool capacity, not a hardcoded 32.
MODEL_CAPABILITIES is directed to extend the existing MODEL_KNOBS / knobs_for() surface rather than duplicate it.
The rule evaluator must be a restricted AST interpreter, never eval(), operating only on resolved run:// values and static literals.
It enumerates file territory as NEW, MODIFY, REUSE, and REFERENCE, with explicit preservation constraints for the suite.py refactor.
standouts: The entire design is framed as a thin driver over an existing node-graph substrate, avoiding a fork.
Injection is a net-new run:// address read, explicitly not free via existing context resolution.
chat_parts is a hot-path refactor of chat() with a long, explicit preservation list for monkeypatch seams.
Concurrency is kept out of the shared scheduler and bounded by the resident model's KV pool, not max_num_seqs.
The rule engine is a deterministic, restricted AST evaluator with structural purity.
conventions: Follows a file-discovered, self-registering registry pattern mirroring existing node-type registration.
Reuses existing substrate pieces (scheduler, store, render, registry, gate predicate precedent) rather than building replacements.
Uses wave-synchronous dispatch with a barrier and serialized store writes.
States binding review corrections inline as negated design decisions (never X).
Breaks from adding concurrency to the shared scheduler; keeps scheduler serial and puts threads in the cognition driver.
Breaks from eval(), mid-generation injection, async/httpx, a separate MODEL_CAPABILITIES surface, and a new cog:// address scheme.
concerns: The hot-path refactor of suite.py chat() has a long, brittle preservation list tied to monkeypatch seams.
Many R1-FOLD corrections are embedded as negations without showing the original error, making audit harder without reading R1-FOLD.
The KV-pool concurrency limit is asserted but not quantified in this file.
Background, sense, and rollup activation triggers are declared net-new but lack concrete integration detail.
The file references line numbers while warning they have drifted, reducing precision.
The symbols list is empty despite dense code references and file territory claims.
notes: A dense, correction-heavy implementation roadmap that tries to keep the cognition layer thin by reusing the existing node-graph substrate and pinning high-risk changes to specific files and seams.
questions: What are the exact contents of review/R1-FOLD.md and review/R2-FOLD.md?
What do the existing node-graph substrate files (scheduler.py, suite.py, registry.py, node_record.py) currently look like?
How is the KV-pool concurrency limit computed for the resident vLLM model?
What is the full lifecycle (GC, persistence, namespacing) of a run:// address?
How do background, sense, and rollup triggers hook into the existing event loop or scheduler?
What are the rhm_* regression suites and how are they executed?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is Document 2 of the triad explaining HOW to make each Completion-Criteria group true, and it delivers grouped implementation guidance (G0–G9) with concrete file targets and corrected decisions.
apparent_themes: Thin drivers on a shared substrate
Mode as a dial for cast, part grain, slot budget, and brain config
Safety through existing governance and an off-MCP swarm
intention_signals: It is trying to add multi-role, multi-part concurrent cognition to the ledger without forking the node-graph engine, using deterministic rules, run://-addressed role outputs, a single resident vLLM-batched model, and staged responses, while preserving existing app behavior and governance.
novelty: It frames concurrent cognition as a thin driver over an existing node graph, uses run:// addresses for inter-role injection, keeps concurrency out of the shared scheduler, and treats mode as a dial that selects cast, part grain, slot budget, and brain config.
contribution: A structured G0–G9 implementation plan that names which substrate pieces to reuse, which files to modify or create, records binding corrected design decisions, and lists explicit preservation constraints for the high-risk suite.py refactor.
summary_for_embedding: Implementation guide for adding a concurrent-cognition layer atop an existing node-graph substrate, organized into G0–G9 work groups with principles, corrected decisions, file territory, and safety gates.
intention_for_embedding: To direct engineering work that makes each Completion-Criteria group true while reusing the node-graph substrate and preserving existing app behavior.
